titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jane Wodening Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 3, 2023 at 06:23

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 23-26 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence, circa 1974-2019. II. Writings, 1969-2018. III. Personal and Family Papers, 1907-2019. IV. Computer Media, undated.

bioghist.head: Jane Wodening (1936-)
bioghist.p: Jane Wodening is an American writer and artist who has published fourteen books, including short story collections, memoirs, and fiction. She is known for her collaborations with experimental filmmaker Stan Brakhage during their marriage from 1957 to 1987, as well as her artistic scrapbooks documenting their early family life in Lump Gulch, Colorado. As the subject of Brakhage's film Window Water Baby Moving (1959), Wodening was filmed giving birth to their first child.

Born Mary Jane Collom in Western Springs, Illinois in 1936, she was known as Jane Brakhage during her marriage, and later adopted the surname Wodening. After separating from Brakhage, she spent the next several years living out of her car before moving to a remote cabin in the Rocky Mountains. These experiences with alternative living became the subject of her memoirs Driveabout (2016) and Living Up There (2009). Wodening began writing in the late 1960s, and self-published her first work, Bird Journal , in 1969; it was later included in her short story collection Mountain Woman Tales (2000). Her other books include her debut biography From the Book of Legends (1989), Lump Gulch Tales (1993), and Brakhage's Childhood (2015), a biography of her former husband.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jane Wodening Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Jane Wodening and Stan Brakhage Scrapbooks (YCAL MSS 229). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
The childhood of Stanley Brakhage : 1410 Quince St., Denver, CO, 2007 / by Jane (Brakhage) Wodening (YCAL MSS 282). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, personal papers, digital files, and other papers by and relating to American writer and artist Jane Wodening. Correspondence includes exchanges with other prominent writers and artists such as Lucia Berlin, Cid Corman, Ed Dorn, Guy Davenport, Merrill Gilfillan, Bobbie Louise Hawkins, Carolee Schneemann, Alec Finlay, and Jonas Mekas. Materials, including notes, drafts, and manuscripts, document Wodening's writing career and provide insight into her creative process. Wodening's personal files showcase her other creative pursuits such as amateur radio and drawing. There are also family records that include a collection of writings by Wodening's brother, poet Jack Collom, and materials relating to her former husband, filmmaker Stan Brakhage. The collection spans the years 1907-2019, with the bulk of the material dating from 1969-2019.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jane Wodening Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of letters and printed email exchanges with writers, poets, artists, filmmakers, family, friends, neighbors, and others. Correspondents include American poets Cid Corman, Ed Dorn, and Michael McClure, American writers Guy Davenport, Merrill Gilfillan, and Bobbie Louise Hawkins, American visual artist Carolee Schneemann, Scottish artist Alec Finlay, and Lithuanian-American filmmaker Jonas Mekas. Some letters to Wodening were enclosed with photographs, clippings, and writings shared by correspondents. The series also contains a large amount of correspondence with American short story writer Lucia Berlin.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: General Correspondence, and Lucia Berlin Correspondence.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series II. Writings, and Series III. Personal and Family Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains incoming and outgoing correspondence of Wodening.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unsorted correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Bartek, Tom, Gloria, Aaron, Ethan, and Quay
Masters, Edgar Lee, "Dippold the Optician" [undated poem]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file does not contain correspondence from Masters (1868-1950). It remains in this series to reflect the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wodening exchanged over 700 letters with Berlin between approximately 1984 and 1994. Many of Berlin's letters are accompanied by typescripts and manuscripts of her short stories, as well as artworks and photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, with typescripts and other works filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Various, including typescript printouts of letters to August Kleinzahler; printed version of The New Censorship ; interview with Eudora Welty by Charlotte Capers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consists of notes, journals, drafts, proofs, research materials, reviews, clippings, correspondence, and related materials for Wodening's published and unpublished short story collections, memoirs, fiction, and other works.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Books, Shorter Works, Notebooks, and Publishing Files.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings can also be found in Series III. Personal and Family Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials pertaining to thirteen of Wodening's published and unpublished books, beginning with her earliest work, Bird Journal , self-published in 1969 and later included in Mountain Woman Tales (2000). Other books include short story collections, memoirs, and fiction, as well as Wodening's biography of former husband, filmmaker Stan Brakhage, Brakhage's Childhood (2015). The subseries consists of manuscript, typescript, and computer printout drafts, many with corrections, as well as notebooks, page proofs, artwork, and correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: Animals I've Neglected to Mention , draft, typescript printouts, corrected, with proofreaders' marks
" Bird Journal , 25 copies," printed version (photocopy), with holograph signature, including "I'm a Writer," printed version, and "Lump Gulch Tales," title page

Draft, typescript printouts, corrected, and background materials

First edition printout (photocopy), with holograph title page

Draft, typescript and photocopy, some corrected
Draft, typescript printout, corrected, with holograph notes
"From the Mountain," draft, typescript printouts, corrected
"From the Mountain," outline and draft, typescript printouts, corrected, with proofreaders' marks by Reed Bye
Holograph notes, with "Eddie the Hunter, His Life," typescript (photocopy)
Draft fragments, holograph and typescript printouts, corrected, with proofreaders' marks; proofreaders' notes
Drafts, holograph and typescript and photocopy, corrected, with annotated pages
Drafts, holograph and typescript and photocopy, corrected, with annotated pages
"The Education of Moses," draft fragments, holograph and typescript printouts, corrected
"Phase 5 Walking and Talking," draft fragments, typescript printouts, corrected
462 page draft, typescript printouts, corrected, with proofreaders' marks
"Wolf's Winter" (working title), drafts, typescript and photocopies, with illustrations
Draft fragments, typescript printouts, corrected, with illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts of short stories, unpublished works, collections of notes, and research material. Short stories in this subseries were filed as stand-alone pieces rather than as part of a full-length book manuscript even if they were included in books, or otherwise appeared in multiple short story collections by Wodening and cannot be assigned to a single title.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three headings: Short Stories, Notes, and Research Materials. Each is arranged alphabetically by file title.

did.unittitle: Binder of typescripts and printouts, including Bird Journal
Card books, holograph and typescript printouts
Draft works, assorted holograph, typescript, and printouts, some corrected
Drafts, assorted holograph, typescript, and photocopy, some corrected
Drafts, assorted holograph typescript, photocopy, and printouts
"I'm a Writer," typescript, signed, with photocopy and corrected copy
"A Matter of Life and Death," typescript (photocopy)
"The Minutes of the Meeting," typescript, with note: "seven copies made"
"My Friend, the Biting Dog," typescript, signed
"On Rabbits and Other Small Creatures," typescripts (photocopy), signed and corrected
Published work, assorted typescript, photocopies, and printouts, some corrected
Unsorted stories, drafts, holograph and typescript
"Weak stories," assorted drafts, holograph, typescript, and photocopy, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains approximately 75 handwritten notebooks, dating from 1974 to 2011, as well as numerous collections of loose sheets of paper that were filed together with the materials.

did.unittitle: Journal of Real Life, holograph notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of business files related to publishing and writing, including query letters, reviews, and Wodening's bibliography.

did.unittitle: Anthologies, with "The Soup Course," drafts, typescript printouts; "The West," page proofs and typescript printouts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series features materials related to Wodening's creative pursuits and hobbies, including amateur radio and drawing, as well as personal materials, such as student papers and photographs, and family records. It also contains files related to Wodening's brother, poet Jack Collom, and her former husband, filmmaker Stan Brakhage.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Subject Files, Personal Papers, and Family Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes artwork, correspondence, clippings, ephemera, notes, photographs, research, and other materials related to Wodening's various creative activities. Subjects include her involvement with volunteer excavations of the Calico Mountains archaeological site in California, documenting spiritual elders of the Lakota Nation, and research about the Teotihuacan Spider Woman. Several files contain Wodening's writings, including drafts and printed versions of short stories and non-fiction work.

did.unittitle: A Calico Primer , printed booklet by Jane Wodening
Materials for "Some Prime Chopping Tools of the Calico Early Man Site" by Ruth DeEtte Simpson and Jane Wodening
Card books, with typescript printouts of various short stories
Gilpin, with "Gilpin Tales Retold," typescript printouts
Pics from Andrew Lang to color
Scrapbook microfiche, with email correspondence from Nancy Kuhl
"What the Iceberg Said at Dawn," holograph and typescript printout
"What the Iceberg Said at Dawn," diagrammed sentence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes Wodening's correspondence, ephemera, student papers, and photographs, as well as a transcript of her amateur radio messages.

did.unittitle: "Travels with Jane: A travel log compiled via amateur radio. Dialogue, monologue, and notes compiled in Dec. 1993 and Jan. 1994. As interpreted by Larry Feick, NF0Z"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of correspondence, historical documents, genealogy records, photographs, and scrapbooks pertaining to Wodening's relatives, the Collom family, and extended Pattee and Jack families, including Wodening's mother, Margaret Collom (née Pattee). Files for Wodening's brother, Jack Collom, contain correspondence and typescripts of his poetry, including numerous self-published books. In addition, there are files for Wodening's former husband, Stan Brakhage, and their children, including correspondence and materials related to his memorial service.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: Collom family, Jack Collom, and Stan Brakhage. Arranged alphabetically by file title.

did.unittitle: Letters to mom, incoming and outgoing
M. Pattee and family correspondence, incoming
"A Book by Taudl and Jack," holograph book "for Myrrena, Crystal, Neowyn, Bearthm, Rarc"
"Conditions and Shouts of Authoritative Poetry," typescript (photocopy), with draft, corrected
"Hauntings of a Live Ghost Town," typescript (photocopy)
"Look at the Pretty Cows, Betty," typescript (photocopy)
"A Note on What This Book is and How to Use It," typescript (photocopy)
"Paragraphs on How to Relate to Nature," typescript (photocopy)
"Poem with Sierra," typescript, with other photocopy poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Wodening's born-digital writing files on 13 flash drives and 170 floppy disks. It also includes electronic media separated from Series III. Personal and Family Papers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
